title: crataegus monogyna var. trilobata
intro: Crataegus monogyna var. trilobata, commonly known as thornless hawthorn, is a variety of the hawthorn plant, Crataegus monogyna. It is a flowering shrub or small tree that is native to Europe and Western Asia, known for its medicinal properties which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and cardiovascular-protective effects. The health benefits of thornless hawthorn include reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and helping to prevent heart disease. The plant is used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
intro_study: According to "Pharmaceutical biology", Crataegus monogyna var. trilobata benefits from having the highest zinc concentration, with 37.21 mg/kg, and a phenolic variety, with neochlorogenic acid as the most abundant, which contributes to its anti-proliferative activity.
